text: BLOCK_M controls token tile height. M = tokens routed to expert. Small M common in decode (few tokens per expert), large M in prefill. Must be multiple of tensor core tile (128 for tcgen05 2SM, 64 for 1SM).
constraints: multiple of 64 for 1SM, 128 for 2SM MMA; CUTLASS ex92 uses 128 (1SM) or 256 (2SM)

text: BLOCK_N controls output hidden dimension tile width. N = hidden dimension of expert. Usually fixed per model (7168 for DeepSeek). Must be multiple of tensor core tile.
constraints: multiple of tensor core atom; CUTLASS ex92 uses 256 for both 1SM and 2SM

text: BLOCK_K controls reduction dimension tile depth. Larger K = fewer mainloop iterations but more smem per stage. For FP8: must be multiple of 16 for tensor core alignment. CUTLASS ex92 uses 128.
constraints: multiple of 16 for FP8; CUTLASS ex92 uses BLOCK_K=128

text: Software pipelining stages for double/multi-buffering. More stages = better memory latency hiding but more smem. Sweet spot usually 3-5 on Blackwell. FlashInfer B200 tuning: uses stage 5 for small M (1-128), stage 3 for medium M (512-4096), stage 1 for large M (2048+).

Number of warps per threadblock. More warps = more parallelism within TB but more register pressure. vLLM uses GROUP_SIZE_M for L2-aware grouping, CUTLASS uses warp-specialized (producer/consumer warps).

Thread block cluster shape for distributed shared memory. 2x1 enables TMA multicast for weight reuse across M tiles. CUTLASS ex92 uses runtime ClusterShape with default 2x1, requires cluster_dim.x >= 2 for 2SM MMA.
constraints: max 16 on B200; must be power of 2; 2SM MMA requires cluster_x >= 2

text: Tensor Memory Accelerator does async bulk copies without using threads. Almost always better on Blackwell. All CUTLASS ex92 variants use TMA (KernelPtrArrayTmaWarpSpecialized). Disable only for very irregular access patterns.
constraints: requires aligned, contiguous memory; TMA alignment = 128 bits

text: Swizzling shared memory layout to avoid bank conflicts during tensor core loads. B128 typically best for FP8. Eliminates bank conflicts in SMEM loads for MMA operands.
constraints: must match tensor core tile expectations

text: Block-wise FP8 scaling group size. Smaller groups = better accuracy but more scale factor loads. 128 is common default. CUTLASS ex92 uses Sm1xxBlkScaledConfig with block-scaled tensor ops (mx_float8_t).
constraints: must divide K evenly; scale layout follows Sm1xxBlkScaledConfig 512B basic-block

text: How tokens are dispatched to experts. Grouped GEMM (CUTLASS ex92 MoEProblemShape) maps directly to hardware. vLLM uses sorted_token_ids with padding to BLOCK_SIZE_M. Permute avoids padding waste but has gather overhead.
constraints: grouped_gemm requires CUTLASS-level API, sorted_tokens works with Triton

text: Fuse activation into GEMM epilogue. SwiGLU (DeepSeek) requires gate+up projection. CUTLASS ex92 supports SiLu fusion via LinCombEltActBlockScaleFactor epilogue. Fusion saves one full write+read of intermediate.
constraints: SwiGLU needs 2x GEMM1 output (gate + up); CUTLASS uses FusionOperation with SiLu

text: Persistent kernel stays resident and picks up new expert tiles from global queue. Helps when many experts with few tokens each (decode). Avoids repeated kernel launch overhead. CUTLASS ex92 uses device-side scheduling.
constraints: requires atomic work queue; more complex synchronization

text: CUTLASS ex92 provides both 1SM and 2SM MMA configs. 1SM: tile 128x256x128, 2SM: tile 256x256x128. 2SM uses cooperative MMA with TMA multicast, 2x compute per tile but requires cluster_x >= 2. Choose based on problem size.
constraints: 2SM requires cluster_shape.x >= 2; may hurt small-M workloads
